notes:
Finished the game almost entirely on Linux, without any flaw or performance issue.

title: DOOM

notes:
It loads a black screen at lightening fast speeds! It gives you at least five minutes of load time so you can listen to your favorite song, make a sandwich, or decide if you want to play a different game, before it even shows you the main menu! And once you finally play a level, you'll be happy to know it runs at a blistering 17 frames per second! It has an "advanced" settings menu, which is there purely for amusement because it doesn't matter if it's on the lowest graphical settings or the highest, the framerate will always dip into single digits at least once a second! If you do literally nothing for the first 30 minutes after a level loads, Steam's overlay will report as much as 200fps! But apparently Steam's overlay is a F*CKING LIAR because Doom still looks like a slideshow!

There was a promising post on github that said VC++ 2015 had to be installed to make Doom not run like hot garbage. I tried installing both it and Doom ON THREE DIFFERENT DISTROS, all with the same exact hardware, and all three gave the exact same results.

I'm giving this a platinum rating because apparently this kind of performance is identical to the Windows version. That's right, trying to play Doom at a comfortable framerate on Windows will make you want to kill yourself there, too. So technically, it's not Proton's fault because Doom is running exactly as Bethesda intended.

It's amazing, isn't it? No indie developer could ever possibly hope to get away with this kind of thing. But because it's Bethesda, Doom is actually on Proton's list of recommendations. What a joke. I would request a refund, but I've spent too much time trying to get this broken mess to work right.

Valve, if you needed the money, you could have just asked. You didn't need to go through all of this work to trick me like this.
title: DOOM

notes:
with driver 418.43,the vulkan performance problem and antialiasing graphics issue seems solved, and an important thing: please uninstall irqbalance in your package manager(it good very bad performance for game) and change swap settings by add this in /etc/sysctl.conf
#swap management
vm.swappiness=10
vm.vfs_cache_pressure=50
it is useful if you have at least 8GB ram(it avoid to put data in swap disk when the main memory is half full (sorry for uggly english)

title: DOOM

notes:
i'll start by saying i'm far from a perfect example of testing, I've never had much interest in doom series, beyond some time messing around the first few levels of the classics i'd say it's not much better then no time. 
this means that i have very little actual experience to equate to for the latest iteration (that i was essntially forced into buying for the 3 hours total time ever put into it, half of which was mp)
going by experience in OTHER games however, it does seem to be working very well, although i did quit only 5 minutes into the intro level since i was feeling increasingly nauseous from what felt like some delay in the mouse input.
loading times are better then windows (scary since i am running this not off the 500gb m.2 ssd but off a old harddrive on usb2.0 of all things.)
the framerate LOOKED good (i've got no fps counter since changeover to fedora).
a statement since i was running it on a 1050ti before, and now it's a r9 270x. and stutter was a serious annoyance. (maybe some sort of smoothing is actually happening, hence the nausea and mouselag)
from a PREVIOUS attempt to get this working i've accidently started the game up with "+com_skipIntroVideo 1 +r_renderapi 1" launch options. so i can't verify how "out of the box" it ran. 
that said, barring my own previous stupidity (ntfs formatted drive originally), i've seen no indiactions of issues despite my own issues.
that could be the "experimental" vulkan support on my card, the age, some sort of issue with frame smoothing... i'll note that ALL games need vsync on my machine, despite tearfree enabled.
that is why i'm ranking it high. as i suspect this experience of mine was more to do with my own system, then the game. it defientely felt far less smooth for me then my previous install under windows. but that was months ago as well.
since i suspect my issues are not the game's, i've ranked it high. due to my own bothced re-install, i can't give it platinum, although i do suspect it has platinum (vaguely remember that proton now sets renderapi itself or something)
(this was NOT tested under beta3.16-8, but 4.2-2, just no signs of a setting for that right now.)
sorry for the ramble.

notes: Hi, me again! I'm the same guy that made the extremely long-winded, sarcastic, and angry report, but still gave it a platinum rating. I've got a huge update. As it turns out, CChromaEditorLibrary.dll is the source of the problem. Apparently it will make the game run like crap, even if you turn off chroma support in the settings. Luckily, some very powerful wizard cast a spell and reversed the hex, releasing the cursed souls from that dll, and all you have to do is download it and overwrite the one steam gives you: https://github.com/Riesi/CChromaEditor/releases All is not well though, as it still takes me at least 5 minutes to load the main menu when starting the game, but hey, it's at least playable now! Again, none of this is Proton's fault. The blame still lays at Bethesda's feet. Proton can't fix lazy and shoddy programming.

title: DOOM

notes:
Runs basically perfect out of the box, almost. I did have to use 500Hz polling rate or lower on the mouse (not sure if they fixed that in Wine or Proton) and I did have to replace the razer chroma dll with a fake dll CChromaEditorLibrary.dll. I don't have razer hardware but replacing that dll made a huge performance increase when the game for some reason thinks i use razer. So i replace it and it disables it, less cpu used and a lot more FPS. The smoothness is amazing and  unbeatable after that. Feels so close to a native Linux or Windows game but even better. 
Here's the github page for the CChromadll fix: https://github.com/Riesi/CChromaEditor/releases/tag/EMPTY1.0 . Then copy that dll to /steamapps/common/DOOM. .steam folder if its on the /home directory to find steamapps (reveal hidden files) or your secondary drive with your steam Library or partition. Replacing that CChroma dll with an empty one is the best tweak to make for this game. 

It is a whitelist worthy game , almost. The CChromaEditorLibrary.dll change is a big deal. 1000hz polling rate, many games tend to stutter with mouse movement, but 500hz is plenty good.

notes:
Game installed no problems and ran first shot. I played for about an hour and a half. 

Graphically the game was great out of the box on max visual settings, got a solid 60 FPS (I'm monitor limited to 60 FPS.)

Audio did not work in the beginning splash / warning videos or in game.

I changed my PulseAudio default sampling rate from 44.1 K to 48 K. This allowed the splash / warning videos to play with sound. The game did not have sound.

I added enabled my default 7.1 HD over HDMI device (I have nothing plugged into it.) and then added a virtual output device for simultaneous output on all local sound cards. I used PulseAudio Preferences graphical utility to do it. This got the sound working in game and out to my speakers (and headset as well if it is on.)

notes:
Runs fantastic on my 4 year old lower mid tier GPU. Playing with the default setting with the exception of FXAA instead of the default AA, and Vulkan instead of OpenGL. It looks beautiful, beautiful as hell actually. Usually around 100 fps, but ranges from 70 to 120 at 1080p.
 I was going to rate this gold instead of platinum, but then I remembered that the fix I did also needs to be done on windows.
The fix. For the first few hours I ran it out of the box and it was fine, but it felt like there was a slight micro stutter. It ran at a constant 60fps with vsync on, but still felt a bit stuttery. The issue stems from the cchroma dll for razor LED keyboards. As others have mentioned, replacing the dll with a forked version from github fixes the microstutter. For me the micro stutter wasn't awful, but it's great to have eliminated the problem.

https://github.com/Riesi/CChromaEditor/releases/tag/EMPTY1.0
(note: the dll file you want is the first link, not the source links below it ;)

title: DOOM

notes:
The game runs with OGL but FPS bounce like crazy. Trying to use Vulkan ingame led to at least 10 crashes at the main screen. By trying "+r_renderAPI 1" without quotes kept the game crashing at start with the crash report stating "+r_renderAPI -2" as the launch option which was kinda odd. Then I tried "+r_renderAPI 0" that launched the game in OGL but I could change to Vulkan without it crashing.

Incredibly strange and that's why I'm giving it a gold report.

Once I was running it on the Vulkan renderer the FPS were stable with my RX 580 8GB pushing 100+ FPS on Ultra at 1440p. Honestly impressed.
